rulings.Case Title: MAURA P. LOREDO VS. ALL AMERICAN FINANCIAL GROUP INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To Set Aside Defaults And Default Judgments On Pltfs Complaint And On The X-Complaint By Option One Mortage Corp And U.S. Bank N.A.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 7, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT AMERICAN GUARDIAN FINANCIAL GROUP, INC. Motion to Set Aside Defaults and Default Judgments on Plaintiff's Complaint and on the Cross-Complaint by Option One Mortage Corp and U.S. Bank N.A. Motion granted as to the cross-complaint. No opposition filed and the parties stipulated. Motion denied as to the complaint. Motion not brought within a reasonable time after entry of default. No excusable neglect shown. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED COMMERCIAL BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SAIGON VILLAGE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Change Venue To Alameda County And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 7, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANTS THOMAS NGUYEN, DIEP LE, SAIGON VILLAGE, LLC Motion to Change Venue to Alameda County. Motion denied. The main relief sought is for recovery of the full amount owed under the loan agreement. The case arises from a underlying breach of contract. The action is transitory, and venue is proper in San Francisco County where the contract was executed. CCP section 395. Also, the venue selection clause is not void, because it does not disrupt statutory venue provisions. (See Alexander vs. Superior Court (2003) 114 Cal.App. 4th 723.)=(302/CWW)
